hero.description: The bridge between "knowing grammar" and "speaking fluently" is a daily 5-minute habit. Stop translating in your head. Start feeling the language.
hero.card_insight: AI Insight: Past tense correction + natural vocabulary.

mission.headline: A Global Mission for Fluency
mission.sub: Education should not be a luxury. STICK is designed to provide high-quality language intuition to everyone, everywhere.
mission.q_desc: Expert-level linguistic feedback powered by state-of-the-art AI, tailored to your personal context.
mission.a_desc: Works on any device, requires only 5 minutes, and costs less than a morning coffee.
mission.s_desc: From individual learners to entire school systems, STICK grows with the user community.

about.headline:
We stopped building courses,
and started building habits.
about.p1: After years of watching students struggle with traditional grammar-first methods, we realized the problem wasn't a lack of information. It was a lack of practice.
about.p2_before: STICK was built on a simple thesis:
about.p2_strong: if we can make practicing English as effortless as scrolling social media
about.p3: We wanted to give you a tool that you actually look forward to opening every day.

psychology.title: The Psychology of Fluency
psychology.subtitle: We built STICK around the brain's natural learning patterns.
psychology.f_desc: 5 minutes is easy to say yes to every single day.
psychology.p_desc: No live teachers means no fear of being judged for mistakes.
psychology.r_desc: You only learn the words you actually want to use.
psychology.rep_desc: Spaced repetition built into your personalized journal.

closing.headline: Ready to rewire your brain?
closing.desc: Join other early adopters who are finally breaking the translation barrier.
closing.trial: 7-day free trial. No credit card required.

target.headline: Designed for the busy and ambitious.
target.group1_desc: Bridge the gap between academic textbook English and real-world fluency.
target.group2_desc: Need to write professional emails and speak naturally in meetings.
target.group3_desc: Want to maintain their English momentum without taking formal classes.
target.group4_desc: Need quick, practical phrasing for daily life in an English-speaking environment.

problem.headline: Why learners study for years and still can't use it.
problem.description: Traditional methods focus on passive consumption. You watch, you read, you listen. But you never produce. The gap between input and output is where fluency goes to die.
problem.card1_desc: Traditional apps force you to study like "The cat goes to school". Zero relevance to your actual life.
problem.card2_desc: You're trained to find the "most grammatically correct" answer, making you speak like a textbook, not a human.
problem.card3_desc: Long sessions are hard to commit to. You binge on Sunday and forget everything by Tuesday.
problem.card4_desc: Boring flashcards don't build emotional connections to the language.

features.polish_desc: STICK doesn't just correct errors; it shows you how a native speaker would express the same thought naturally.
features.journal_desc: Low pressure. High output. Just tell STICK about your day. No prompts, no rules—just your life in English.

validation.test_quote: "I finally stopped translating in my head before speaking. The words just flow naturally now."

step1.title: Write tiny daily thoughts
step1.description: A few short lines each day are enough.
step1.info: You can change your reminder settings later.

step2.title: Get instant AI help
step2.description: See a more natural version of what you wrote. Our AI understands your intent and suggests human-like alternatives instantly.
step2.quote: The art of language is not in being perfect, but in being understood.

step3.title: Practice saying it aloud
step3.description: Listen, repeat, and build confidence step by step.
step3.tip: Pro tip: Record yourself to hear the difference

step4.title: Build a real habit
step4.description_line1: Tiny daily practice beats long rare sessions.

footer.tagline: Drawn with intention. Built for thinkers. The analog sanctuary for digital learning.
footer.subscribe_desc: Get the weekly manifesto on learning.

a_lot_of.meaning: A large amount or number of something.
a_lot_of.example: I have a lot of friends.

journal_workspace.placeholder: Just start writing — it doesn't have to be perfect...
journal_workspace.stuck_label: Not sure where to start? Try these:
journal_workspace.quick_start_3: One thing I want to change is...
journal_workspace.prompt_goals: What do you want to do tomorrow?
journal_workspace.motivation_desc: Just {{target}} words is enough to keep your streak today. You've got this!
journal_workspace.stick_feedback_empty: Go ahead and start writing! I'll give you feedback as you go. No worries about mistakes — that's exactly how you learn.
journal_workspace.stick_feedback_typing: Nice work! You've written {{count}} words so far. Just a bit more to hit {{target}}!
journal_workspace.tip: Try using "because" to connect your ideas — it makes your sentences flow more naturally!

vocab_suggest.hang_out: To talk or spend time with friends

feedback_loading.title: Generating your feedback...

feedback_loading.quote: Every entry is a step toward fluency.

feedback_result.uncountable_desc: "Homework" doesn't take an 's'. Use "a lot of" or "much" instead of "many".
feedback_result.past_tense_desc: Since you started with "Yesterday," use "saw" instead of "see" for consistency.
feedback_result.flow_desc: "Headed home" sounds more natural in casual conversation than just "go home".
feedback_result.word_headed: To move in a specific direction.
feedback_result.word_fair_amount: Another way to say "quite a lot".
feedback_result.encouragement: Your storytelling is getting much clearer. Try speaking this version out loud to lock in the rhythm.
feedback_result.streak_hint: One more entry to hit your 7-day streak!

lesson_detail.title: Basic Tenses
lesson_detail.subtitle: Master present, past, and future tenses.
lesson_detail.intro_content: English has 12 basic tenses. They help you express time — when something happens, happened, or will happen. Let's start with the three simplest ones.
lesson_detail.examples_content: See how tenses change meaning: 'I walk to school' (habit) vs 'I walked to school' (yesterday) vs 'I will walk to school' (tomorrow).
lesson_detail.practice_content: Fill in the blanks with the correct tense form. Pay attention to time markers like 'yesterday', 'now', and 'tomorrow'.
lesson_detail.summary_content: Remember: Simple Present = habits/facts, Simple Past = finished actions, Simple Future = plans/predictions.
lesson_detail.quiz_q1: Choose the correct form: 'She ___ to school every day.'
lesson_detail.quiz_q2: Choose the correct form: 'They ___ the movie last night.'

saved_phrases.title: Saved Phrases
saved_phrases.subtitle: Your collection of useful expressions.
saved_phrases.m_break_ice: To initiate conversation in a social setting
saved_phrases.m_other_hand: Used to present an alternative viewpoint
saved_phrases.m_long_run: Over a long period of time; eventually
saved_phrases.m_nail_head: To describe exactly what is causing a situation
saved_phrases.m_not_only: Used to add emphasis to a connected pair of ideas
saved_phrases.footer_quote: Words are, of course, the most powerful drug used by mankind.

vocab_notebook.title: Vocabulary Notebook
vocab_notebook.subtitle: Track and master your word collection.
vocab_notebook.def_eloquent: Fluent or persuasive in speaking or writing
vocab_notebook.ex_eloquent: She gave an eloquent speech at the conference.
vocab_notebook.def_serendipity: The occurrence of events by chance in a happy way
vocab_notebook.ex_serendipity: Finding that book was pure serendipity.
vocab_notebook.ex_ubiquitous: Smartphones have become ubiquitous in modern life.
vocab_notebook.def_resilient: Able to recover quickly from difficulties
vocab_notebook.def_contemplate: To look at thoughtfully; to consider
vocab_notebook.ex_contemplate: She sat by the window, contemplating her next move.
vocab_notebook.empty_desc: Tap + to add your first word!

static_pages.terms_s1_content: By accessing or using STICK, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, please do not use our services.
static_pages.terms_s2_content: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials. You agree to provide accurate information and to keep it up to date.
static_pages.terms_s3_content: All content, features, and functionality of STICK are owned by STICK Analog EdTech and are protected by international copyright laws.
static_pages.privacy_s1_content: We collect information you provide directly: your name, email, language level, and journal entries. We also collect usage data to improve the learning experience.
static_pages.privacy_s2_content: Your data is used to personalize your learning experience, provide feedback on your writing, and track your progress. We never sell your personal data.
static_pages.privacy_s3_content: We implement industry-standard security measures to protect your personal information. Your journal entries are encrypted at rest and in transit.
static_pages.help_s1_content: Navigate to the Journal section from the sidebar or bottom navigation. Choose a prompt or free-write, then submit your entry for AI-powered feedback.
static_pages.help_s2_content: Your entries are scored on grammar, vocabulary, coherence, and effort. The AI provides detailed feedback with specific suggestions for improvement.
static_pages.help_s3_title: Can I change my language level?
static_pages.help_s3_content: Yes! Go to Settings from your Profile page. You can adjust your level at any time, and prompts will adapt to match your current ability.
static_pages.about_s1_content: STICK was born from a simple belief: the best way to learn a language is to use it every day. We bridge the gap between 'knowing grammar' and 'thinking in English'.
static_pages.about_s2_content: In a world of flashy apps, STICK embraces the beauty of handwritten notes, sketch borders, and intentional design. Learning should feel human, not mechanical.
static_pages.about_s3_content: STICK is designed for reflective learners who want depth over speed. Every feature encourages thoughtful practice over rote memorization.
